title: <name> and the Magic Wand and the Moonlight Wish

pages.text: One soft, starry night, <name> was playing in her garden under the big mango tree. The moon shone bright, and fireflies twinkled all around. Suddenly — plink! — something shiny fell into the grass.

<name> picked it up. 'A wand!' she whispered. It sparkled with silver stars. She gave it a little wave. Twinkle! Flash! — the stars began to dance! The flowers started humming a sleepy tune, la-la-laaa, and the butterflies glowed like tiny lamps.
‘Oh my!’ giggled <name>. ‘It’s real magic!’ A talking rabbit hopped out from behind a bush. 'Don’t wave too fast!' he said. 'Last time, I turned purple!' <name> laughed and waved again — swish!

The pond turned into a shiny mirror, and a sleepy owl wearing a golden crown blinked at his reflection. 'Hoo-hoo! I look royal!' he said proudly. The trees giggled, the wind sang softly, and the stars floated down like glittery snow.

It felt like the whole garden was dreaming. <name> looked up at the moon and whispered, 'I wish everyone in the world could smile tonight.' The wand sparkled one last time, sending tiny heart-shaped lights into the sky.
‘Goodnight, little magician,’ said the rabbit. <name> smiled, tucked the wand under her pillow, and drifted off to sleep as the moonlight whispered, 'Sweet dreams, <name>.'
